news.summary: New York & Tel Aviv-based Pagaya Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ: PGY) revealed its financial performance for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. The company, known for providing artificial intelligence infrastructure to the financial industry, expressed optimism about its profitability in the current year. Pagaya emphasized its commitment to enhancing profits and generating value for its shareholders. For more details, the company's first quarter 2025 shareholder letter can be accessed.

news.summary: New data from the BROADWAY and TANDEM studies was unveiled at EAS 2025 and released in prestigious medical journals. The findings were published in The New England Journal of Medicine for BROADWAY and in The Lancet for TANDEM. This simultaneous release has sparked interest in the medical community.
news.headline: New data from BROADWAY and TANDEM studies published in top medical journals and presented at EAS Congress 2025.

news.summary: Several crypto-focused organizations, including Bitcoin mining companies, are considering a return to the US due to geopolitical tensions. However, Hive Digital Technologies is focusing on the Latin American market, particularly Paraguay, for its long-term potential. The company acquired Bitfarms' 200MW facility in Paraguay for $56 million and completed a 100MW data center in April, aiming to expand to 300MW by 2025 and increase hashrate to 25 EH/s. Despite concerns over a proposed mining ban in Paraguay, Hive is working with policymakers to support mining legislation. The company is also diversifying globally in Canada, Sweden, and the US to mitigate geopolitical risks and secure sourcing for ASICs and electrical components. Hive's CEO views Bitcoin mining profitability as a physics equation, focusing on controlling operational expenses, power costs, and machine uptime to drive predictable outputs. The company is scaling operations by securing ASIC orders, long-term power access, and expanding engineering capacity across three continents to navigate market uncertainties.
news.headline: HIVE partners with Paraguay for energy, acquires 200MW facility for $56M, plans 300MW by 2025. Aims for 25EH/s by Sept. Diversifying globally.

news.summary: A company named Aeson Power recently unveiled its latest sodium products at EES Europe 2025, a prominent event in the energy storage sector. Among the products showcased were the SIBPOM-4850 designed for telecommunications, SIBPOM-12100 meant for UPS, and the SIBPOM-125kWh energy storage cabinet for C&I-scale applications. These products offer advanced solutions for various industries, demonstrating the company's innovation and commitment to the energy storage market. The event in Munich attracted a significant number of industry professionals, with attendees eager to learn about these cutting-edge products.
news.headline: Aeson Power presents sodium battery tech at EES Europe 2025, including SIBPOM-4850, SIBPOM-12100, and SIBPOM-125kWh cabinet for C&I, showcased in Munich.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, in Washington, the National Shooting Sports Foundation (NSSF), an association representing the firearm industry, commended Tennessee Governor Bill Lee for enacting SB 1360. This new law enhances safeguards for the firearm sector and upholds Second Amendment rights in Tennessee. The legislation ensures the protection of the state's gun industry and reinforces citizens' rights to bear arms.
news.headline: NSSF praises TN Gov. Lee for signing SB 1360 to protect firearm industry & Second Amendment rights in the state.

news.summary: By the end of March, the company boasted a total of approximately 11.7 million subscribers. There was a significant 21.9 percent increase in adjusted operating profit from the previous year's same quarter.

news.summary: Market futures experienced an increase as key Trump administration officials are set to engage in discussions with Chinese counterparts this week. On Tuesday, Palantir stocks declined, while Robinhood stocks surged. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's upcoming statements are highly anticipated. The Dow Jones futures saw a rise, reflecting positive sentiments towards the upcoming U.S.-China trade negotiations and Powell's imminent address.
news.headline: Dow Jones futures up on US-China trade talks, meeting with Chinese officials, Palantir slides while Robinhood climbs. Fed Chief Powell important.

A Ukrainian drone attacked the Church of the Great Martyr Demetrius of Thessaloniki in Kursk's Khomutovsky district, Khinshtein reported. The attack damaged the building's glazing and roof, and caused smoke without a fire. There are no casualties or injuries.

TSOLife aims to revolutionize senior living communities by leveraging person-centered data and cutting-edge AI tools. This substantial investment is expected to fuel their mission of enhancing the quality of life for seniors through innovative technology solutions.

news.summary: Tripadvisor, Inc. released its first quarter 2025 earnings report today, May 7, 2025. The report can be accessed on the company's Investor Relations website and the SEC website. A conference call discussing the results is scheduled for 8:30 a.m. ET today. For further details, the complete news is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached 6.7%, with a total of 10.2 million people currently unemployed. This is a significant increase from last year, when the rate was at 4.3%. The labor force participation rate has also decreased to 61.4%, down from 63.2% last year. Additionally, it was reported that the economy grew by 2.3% in the last quarter, which is lower than the projected 3% growth. Experts attribute these numbers to the ongoing impact of the global pandemic on the economy.
news.headline: Uber's quarterly bookings fall short of expectations as rideshare growth decelerates. - Report

news.summary: According to Ming Wu, the founder of RabbitX, the future of decentralized finance (DeFi) involves expanding perpetual futures trading into real-world assets, stocks, and interest rate derivatives. In an interview with Benzinga, Wu shared his vision for the evolution of perpetuals in the coming years, focusing on increasing accessibility and introducing a wider range of asset classes and liquidity enhancements. Wu expressed excitement about the possibility of including assets such as RWAs, commodities, interest rates, and stocks in perpetual trading, which would offer new trading and hedging opportunities for DeFi users. RabbitX, a decentralized derivatives exchange, is at the forefront of driving this innovation.
news.headline: RabbitX founder plans to expand perpetual futures trading to real-world assets and stocks for more diverse trading options.

news.summary: In a pioneering move towards brand development and industry advancement, Horizon Media Holdings has appointed Crystal Park to lead the charge. The parent company of the largest media agency in the U.S. and the top independent globally made the announcement on May 7, 2025. Crystal Park's expertise in marketing and ad technology is set to propel brand growth and shape the industry's evolution moving forward.
news.headline: Crystal Park named CMO of Horizon Media Holdings, overseeing brand growth. Horizon Media is US's largest media agency.

news.summary: Vernon Hills, Illinois - CDW Corporation (Nasdaq: CDW), a prominent provider of IT solutions to various sectors, including business, government, education, and healthcare in the US, UK, and Canada, disclosed that its Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.625 per common share. The dividend is scheduled to be disbursed on June 10, 2025, to shareholders of record as of May 26, 2025. It was noted that forthcoming dividends will be contingent on certain conditions.
news.headline: CDW declares quarterly dividend of $0.625/share to be paid on June 10, 2025 to stockholders of record as of May 26.

news.summary: Hagerty, Inc., a specialty vehicle insurance provider, reported strong financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The Total Revenue increased by 18% year-over-year to $319.6 million, with Written Premium also rising by 12% to $244.3 million. Marketplace revenue surged by 176% to $29.0 million, and Operating Income increased by 110% to $25.7 million. Net Income saw a significant rise of 233% to $27.3 million, with Adjusted EBITDA increasing by 45% to $39.6 million. The company reaffirmed its 2025 outlook for 12-13% Total Revenue growth, 30-40% Net Income growth, and 21-29% Adjusted EBITDA growth. Hagerty's strategic priorities and investments in technology aim to drive efficient customer service and accelerate growth. Cash reserves were at $128 million with total debt at $147 million, $32 million of which is back leverage for Broad Arrow Capital's loan portfolio. The company anticipates sustained profit growth in 2025, with investments in technology platforms like Duck Creek to fuel future business growth. Hagerty seeks to fuel car culture and deliver value to stakeholders, focusing on compounding revenue growth through its insurance, membership, and marketplace businesses. The company's 2025 outlook includes Written Premium growth of 13-14%, Total Revenue growth of 12-13%, Net Income growth of 30-40%, and Adjusted EBITDA growth of 21-29%. Hagerty aims to invest in technology while maintaining profitability and growth. The full financial results and outlook are available along with a detailed press release on Hagerty's investor relations website. Hagerty, Inc. continues to be a prominent brand and provider in the automotive enthusiast industry, with a commitment to sustaining growth and delivering value to its customers and shareholders.
news.headline: Hagerty reports Q1 2025 results with revenue up 18% to $319.6 million and net income up 233% to $27.3 million. Reaffirms outlook for 2025: 12-13% revenue growth, 30-40% net income growth, and 21-29% EBITDA growth.Margins expanded and efficient tech investments made. Hagerty anticipates profiting from car culture and driving. TRAVERSE CITY, Mich., May 7, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Full story available on Benzinga.com

news.summary: RumbleOn, Inc. reported their first-quarter results for 2025, with a revenue of $244.7 million showing a decrease of 20.5% compared to the same period in 2024. The net loss improved to $9.7 million from $10.3 million. Operating cash outflows were $6.9 million compared to inflows of $17.0 million in 2024. The company repaid $38.8 million in convertible senior notes. Powersports segment saw a decline in unit sales, with new retail unit sales down 23.7% and pre-owned down 13.9%. The total powersports unit sales declined by 20.5%. Gross profit in the powersports segment was $66.1 million, down 16.4%. Adjusted EBITDA declined to $7.0 million from $7.7 million. Cash on hand was $41.1 million, down from $85.3 million. Long-term debt also decreased to $215.5 million. Full story available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: RumbleOn's Q1 2025 revenue: $244.7M (20.5% decrease YoY), net loss improved to $9.7M, SG&A expense $61.1M.

news.summary: Ceragon, a wireless connectivity solutions provider, reported Q1 2025 financial results with revenues of $88.7 million, up 0.2% from the previous year. Operating income was ($1.1) million on a GAAP basis and $4.5 million on a non-GAAP basis. Net income was ($1.0) million on GAAP and $2.6 million on non-GAAP basis. India saw highest bookings since Q1 2024, North America bookings improved, and EMEA rebounded based on African strength. Cash and equivalents were $27.7 million. Management reiterated 2025 outlook with revenue of $390-$430 million. Conference call available for details. Ceragon serves 600+ service providers in 130+ countries with innovative wireless solutions. Note forward-looking statements and potential risks. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Ceragon Networks' Q1 2025 revenues were $88.7M, with highest bookings since Q1 2024; net income was $2.6M. 2025 outlook: Revenue $390M-$430M, aiming for 10% non-GAAP operating margin. Cash was $27.7M. Agencies worldwide rely on Ceragon's 5G/4G wireless solutions in 130+ countries. ROI detailed: $29.1M gross profit, 32.8% margin (vs 36.2% in Q1 2024); $4.5M operating profit. Expect free cash flow growth in 2025. Financial results provided in comparative tables; live conference available and replay on website. For more info, visit Ceragon's official site.

news.summary: Endo, Inc. reported a 6% decrease in total revenues for Q1 2025 compared to Q1 2024, with net loss at $129 million for Q1 2025, down from $154 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2025 was $99 million, lower than $146 million in Q1 2024. Branded Pharmaceuticals saw revenues of $209 million in Q1 2025, up 4% from Q1 2024, driven by XIAFLEX's 7% revenue growth. Sterile Injectables revenues dropped to $71 million in Q1 2025 from $98 million in Q1 2024 due to competition. The Company reaffirmed its 2025 revenue guidance of $1,775 to $1,860 million and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $620 to $650 million. Endo is expected to merge with Mallinckrodt in the second half of 2025 and divest its International Pharmaceuticals business by mid-2025.
news.headline: Endo's Q1 2025: XIAFLEX revenues up 7%, reaffirms 2025 guidance of $1,775-$1,860 million revenue and $620-$650 million adjusted EBITDA.

news.summary: Vistra Corp reported a GAAP net loss of $(268) million and cash flow from operations of $599 million for the first quarter of 2025. The ongoing operations net loss was $(200) million with adjusted EBITDA at $1,240 million. The company reaffirmed its 2025 guidance ranges for adjusted EBITDA of $5.5-$6.1 billion and adjusted FCFbG of $3.0-$3.6 billion. As of May 2, 2025, Vistra had executed $5.2 billion in share repurchases, reducing shares outstanding by 30%. The company had liquidity of $3,903 million as of March 31, 2025. Vistra continues to invest in clean energy resources, with ongoing projects in solar and energy storage. The company also mentioned its commitment to providing reliable and affordable electricity while focusing on sustainability.
news.headline: Vistra reports first quarter 2025 results with a Net Loss of $(268) million, Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA of $1,240 million. Company affirms 2025 guidance ranges for Adjusted EBITDA, FCFbG. Share repurchases of $5.2 billion executed, leaving $1.5 billion available. Continued clean energy investments including solar and energy storage projects. Total liquidity reported as $3,903 million as of March 31, 2025. Vistra remains focused on innovation, sustainability, and long-term value creation.

news.summary: In King of Prussia, Pennsylvania on May 7, 2025, Vertex, Inc. (NASDAQ: VERX), a prominent global supplier of indirect tax services, revealed its financial performance for the quarter ended on March 31, 2025.
news.headline: Vertex Inc. (NASDAQ: VERX) reports Q1 2025 financial results.

news.summary: In Wilmington, Delaware, positive high-level findings from the DESTINY-Breast11 Phase III trial revealed that ENHERTU® (fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki) followed by paclitaxel, trastuzumab, and pertuzumab (THP) showcased a statistically significant and clinically meaningful enhancement in pathologic complete response (pCR) rate compared to the standard of care (dose-dense doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide followed by THP [ddAC-THP]) when administered in the neoadjuvant setting in patients. This signifies a notable advancement in treatment with promising outcomes for individuals undergoing neoadjuvant therapy, emphasizing the potential benefits of this new approach.
news.headline: ENHERTU® showed significant results in breast cancer patients with high-risk HER2-positive tumors, improving pathologic complete response.

news.summary: Bitcoin holders are more willing to borrow against their crypto assets as market confidence grows, says Xapo Bank CEO, Seamus Rocca. Bitcoin is currently at $95,000, with institutional adoption increasing. Xapo Bank recently launched a lending product allowing users to borrow up to $1 million in US dollars using their Bitcoin as collateral. Rocca mentioned expectations for wider institutional adoption and long-term thinking driving demand. Xapo Bank offers loan-to-value (LTV) ratios of 20%, 30%, and 40%, with borrowers able to access liquidity without selling their Bitcoin. Borrowing against Bitcoin helps holders avoid selling during emergencies, providing upside potential while obtaining liquidity. The Xapo Bank executive predicts more long-term holders will utilize crypto liquidity without selling their BTC.
news.headline: Xapo Bank CEO sees Bitcoin-backed loans as natural move with Bitcoin near $95,000; offers up to $1 million loans with 20%-40% LTV ratios.

news.summary: A contract has been awarded to QinetiQ US by Integris Composites to assist the U.S. Army's Future Long Range Assault Aircraft (FLRAA) program. This agreement involves QinetiQ US providing systems engineering and integration services. The contract was announced in McLean, Va., on May 7, 2025. The program aims to enhance the U.S. Army's capabilities through the development of the FLRAA, and QinetiQ's expertise with LAST Armor® Integration will play a critical role. This partnership signifies an important step forward in bolstering national defense, with significant implications for the defense sector.
news.headline: QinetiQ US awarded contract to support U.S. Army's FLRAA program for survivability solutions.

news.summary: According to Broadridge, firms are at risk of increased cyber threats due to reliance on outdated legacy systems. The SIFMA Ops 2025 conference in New York on May 7, 2025, highlighted rising worries about operational and cyber resilience. Key figures at the event discussed the pressing need for modernization to address these challenges. This underscores the urgency for companies to update their systems to mitigate the growing cyber risks they face.
news.headline: Financial services industry faces growing concerns over operational and cyber resilience due to outdated systems. SIFMA Ops 2025 conference highlighted the urgency with experts discussing strategies to mitigate risks.

news.summary: In Toronto, Adastra Corporation has been honored with the esteemed 2025 Canada’s Best Managed Companies award for its outstanding performance, global business practices, and consistent growth. This recognition is part of the Canada’s Best Managed Companies program, which commends excellence in private Canadian-owned companies generating revenues of $50 million or more. This achievement comes as Adastra marks over 30 years in operation. Companies are assessed for this designation based on their leadership in strategy, culture, and other essential aspects.
news.headline: Adastra Corp receives 2025 Best Managed Companies award in Canada for 23 years; companies must have $50M+ revenue.

news.summary: In Billerica, Massachusetts, Bruker Corporation (Nasdaq: BRKR) released its financial results for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company's President and CEO, Frank H. Laukien, noted a strong beginning to the year with double-digit reported and CER revenue growth. The BSI segment saw a notable 5.1% organic revenue increase, exceeding operating margin expectations despite market uncertainties. Throughout Q1 and April, Bruker successfully introduced significant new products, showcasing robust performance amidst challenging conditions.
news.headline: Bruker Corp. reports strong Q1 2025 results with double-digit revenue growth, 5.1% organic growth in BSI segment, and better-than-expected operating margin.

news.summary: A partnership has been formed between LambdaTest and MacStadium, a private Mac cloud provider, to enhance AI-native software testing using Apple Silicon. LambdaTest, a unified AI and cloud engineering platform, will leverage MacStadium's infrastructure to accelerate their testing capabilities. Through their AI-driven testing agent, KaneAI, LambdaTest aims to revolutionize test automation processes, enabling teams to streamline software testing through natural language commands. This collaboration marks an important step in advancing software testing methodologies, particularly for enterprise macOS workloads.
news.headline: LambdaTest partners with MacStadium to use Apple Silicon for AI testing, enhances automation.

news.summary: OppFi Inc. (NYSE: OPFI) based in Chicago, a notable digital finance platform collaborating with banks to offer financial services to everyday Americans, disclosed its financial performance for the period ending March 31, 2025. The CEO of OppFi, Todd Schwartz, expressed satisfaction with the results, citing a remarkable first quarter achievement. The company recorded its highest quarterly revenue and adjusted net income figures to date. This achievement signifies a significant milestone for OppFi as they continue to grow in the realm of providing financial solutions to the public.
news.headline: OppFi (OPFI) reports record Q1 revenue exceeding guidance by over 40%, raising full-year adjusted net income & EPS. CEO pleased.

news.summary: In the latest financial report, Rockwell Automation, Inc. (NYSE: ROK) announced the results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025. The company displayed robust operational performance with sales, margins, and EPS surpassing projections. There was a significant influx of orders across various business segments, leading to a total company book-to-bill ratio aligning with the historical average of around 1.0.
